examples: His poor method of learning by rote made him unable to deal with the problems in his work flexibly.
Plant communities assemble themselves flexibly, and their particular structure depends on the specific history of the area.
By law, companies must "consider seriously" requests to work flexibly made by a parent with a child under the age of six, or a disabled child under 18.
People who work flexibly can have more job satisfaction, be healthier and more productive.
Users at the same time are mobile and can access their information and entertainment more flexibly.
